explanation: The sentence describes a condition under which a Recruitment Maneuver (RM) can be initiated. First, 'In PCV and PSV mode' translates to the disjunction 'PCVMode | PSVMode'. 'at the end of an inspiration' translates to 'inspiratoryPhaseEnd'. 'inspiratory pause is not required' translates to '!inspiratoryPause'. These three conditions form the premise: '(PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause'. The consequence is 'it shall be possible to initiate a lung recruitment procedure, termed Recruitment Maneuver (RM), if it is required by the GUI'. This means if 'RMButton' (required by GUI) is true, then 'RM' (Recruitment Maneuver) should eventually become true. This translates to 'RMButton -> F RM'. The entire statement describes a general rule that should always hold, so it is wrapped by the global operator 'G'. Thus, the final formula is G(((PCVMode | PSVMode) & inspiratoryPhaseEnd & !inspiratoryPause) -> (RMButton -> F RM)).
